text:
is a character that appears in the Game Boy Advance Game Fullmetal Alchemist: Sonata of Memories.

Etymology

His first name, Claves, refers to a percussion instrument consisting of two short wooden stick which are hit together to produce sound.
His surname is a borrowed loanword in Japanese which means Bassoon in English, which is an instrument that is part of the woodwind family. As his name has never been officially romanized into English, it is unclear which language his name is meant to originate from, although it could reasonably translate into any of the following spellings:
In German, this instrument is called a Fagott.
In Italian, this instrument is called a Fagotto.
In Czech, Danish, Dutch, Polish, Romanian, and Spanish, this instrument is called a Fagot.
If the meaning of his surname was translated literally, his name could also be translated into English as Claves Bassoon.
If left untranslated, the direct romanisation of his Japanese name, ファゴット, would be Fagotto as well.

Trivia

Both his forename and surname are taken from musical instruments. His alchemy pupil, Lute Zaxophone, follows a similar naming scheme.
His first name, Claves, can be pronounced in two different ways in English. One is two syllables, said like "CLAW-vase", and the second is one syllable, pronounced as it's spelt (rhyming with Caves). Due to how foreign words are translated into Japanese, both pronunciations would ultimately be spelt as クラベス Kurabesu in Japanese, making it unclear how his name should be pronounced in English.
In the English fan translation, his first name was translated as Cravis (which is a mistranslation) while his surname was translated as Fagott.
